methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Intramotev is a Series A stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector that has raised $14.4M to date. The broader sector comprises eight private peers including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Rainmaker, with no public comparables available for direct benchmarking.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable and forms the primary valuation anchor. Using total capital raised of $14.4M as a proxy for current enterprise value and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage advancement yields $28.8M. A peer-median approach cannot be reliably applied; while four same-sector peers exist, none satisfy the narrow filters of same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar valuation tier simultaneously, falling short of the three-datapoint minimum threshold required for statistical validity.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $24M to $33M, with a median point of $29M, derived from stage-progression methodology alone. Key uncertainties include the absence of tight peer comparables at the same stage and valuation tier—limiting cross-validation of the stage-progression result—and the company's specific competitive positioning within the autonomous systems landscape relative to better-capitalized peers. Additional financial and operational metrics would strengthen confidence in this range.
